name: Apricot Nectar Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 package Yellow cake mix (regular size), without pudding in the mix
2 tbsp Tub style reduced calorie Margarine
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Lightly coat a 12-cup Bundt Pan with non-stick spray and dust the pan with 1 tablespoon of flour. Set aside.
3. In a large bowl, beat the egg whites with an electric mixer until foamy. Stir in the applesauce, then stir in the cake mix and sugar.
4. Slowly drizzle the apricot nectar into the egg white mixture while beating on low speed. Beat on medium speed for 5 minutes. Pour batter into prepared pan.
5. Bake for 45 to 50 minutes or until a toothpick inserted near center comes out clean. Cool in pan on rack for 10 minutes.
6. GLAZE: In a small saucepan, stir together sugar, buttermilk, margarine and vanilla. Bring to boil without stirring. Reduce heat and gently boil for one minute.
7. Invert cake onto serving platter and remove pan. Puncture cake while warm with toothpick. Pour glaze over cake. Cool before serving.
8. Store covered in refrigerator.
To prevent false-positive safety triggers, avoid aggressive culinary terms like "kill the heat," "smash," "destroy," "burn," "splutter," or "execute" when describing cooking steps.
